title: Units for Target Income
event: Reyes Co. has fixed costs of $120,000 and a $20 contribution margin per unit. Management wants pretax income of $60,000. Compute the required unit sales.

computationPaths.narration: Target income is just more mountain to climb — stack it on top of fixed costs and divide by the same CM.

memorize.body: Units for target = (Fixed costs + target income) ÷ CM per unit.
For an AFTER-tax target, gross it up first: pretax = after-tax ÷ (1 − tax rate), then run the same formula.

questions.prompt: FC $120,000, CM $20/unit, target income $60,000. Required units = ?

distractors.feedback: That's plain break-even — you dropped the target. The 60,000 goal needs 3,000 more contributions.
That covers ONLY the target income. Fixed costs must be buried first: (120 + 60) ÷ 20 = 9,000.
